pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run against Java 17 specifications, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot from 2.7.x to 3.3.x to support Java 21 and Jakarta EE.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline and uses javax.* packages, causing runtime failures or incompatibility with Java 21/Jakarta EE 9+ environments.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Cloud to a version compatible with Spring Boot 3.x.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Cloud 2021.x is incompatible with Spring Boot 3.x, leading to dependency resolution conflicts and runtime errors.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated javax.annotation-api with Jakarta-compatible annotation handling or remove explicit dependency if provided by platform.
pom.xml.why_exists: javax.annotation-api is removed from the JDK and often incompatible with Jakarta EE 9+ environments used by Spring Boot 3; explicit removal prevents classpath conflicts.

pom.xml.general_idea: Remove explicit JAXB dependencies (javax.xml.bind) as they are replaced by Jakarta XML Binding or handled differently in Spring Boot 3.
pom.xml.why_exists: javax.xml.bind is not available in Java 11+ without explicit inclusion, and Spring Boot 3 expects Jakarta XML Binding (jakarta.xml.bind); keeping javax versions causes NoClassDefFoundError.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update JAXB version properties to align with Jakarta XML Binding or compatible implementations for Spring Boot 3.
pom.xml.why_exists: Old JAXB versions (javax) are incompatible with the Jakarta namespace required by Spring Boot 3; updating versions ensures correct artifact resolution.

pom.xml.general_idea: Switch Maven compiler plugin configuration from source/target to release flag for Java 9+ compatibility.
pom.xml.why_exists: Using source/target with Java 21 can lead to mismatched module path and classpath behavior; the release flag is the standard for Java 9+ to ensure correct boot classpath usage.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Lombok to a version that supports Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older Lombok versions may fail to compile or process annotations correctly on Java 21 due to internal API changes.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add lombok-mapstruct-binding dependency to ensure compatibility between Lombok and MapStruct in the annotation processor path.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this binding, MapStruct may fail to resolve Lombok-generated getters/setters during compilation, causing build failures.
